libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use `Conscrypt.checkAvailability()` before exercising APIs and create or install a provider with `Conscrypt.newProvider()` / `Security.insertProviderAt(...)` before requesting JCA/JSSE services. Preserve the existing test/native-image JVM opens for Java 9+ internals: `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`, `--add-opens=java.base/java.net=ALL-UNNAMED`, and `--add-opens=java.base/sun.nio.ch=ALL-UNNAMED`.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8879 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.conscrypt:conscrypt-openjdk-uber:1.4.1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The uber artifact already embeds native libraries under `META-INF/native`; failures are more likely reachability/JNI/resource metadata issues than missing dependencies.
Tests that exercise real sockets or SSLEngine paths may require loopback networking but not an external Docker service.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Conscrypt openjdk-uber is self-contained and needs no extra Maven dependency or Docker service, but meaningful tests must initialize the Conscrypt provider and preserve module opens used for its JDK-internal socket reflection.
